channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Rejects India’s Criticism of Its Counterterrorism Operations in Afghanistan
item.description: On 15th March 2026, Pakistan's Foreign Office (FO) strongly rejected a statement issued by the Indian Ministry of External Affairs that criticised Pakistan’s cross-border operations in Afghanistan. In...

item.title: African Bank Experiences Multiple CEO Departures Amid Governance Concerns
item.description: African Bank has seen yet another change in leadership, with Kennedy Bungane stepping down as CEO, marking the third departure under Chairman Thabo Dloti. In the interim, Zweli Manyathi, who heads the...

item.title: Hannah Gadsby Discusses Woof!, Trivial Pursuit, and Unexpected Fans
item.description: Australian comedian Hannah Gadsby recently released a free stand-up special on YouTube called Woof!, where she performs as a sock puppet. Gadsby explains the choice as a way to step back from being on...

item.title: Kannywood Actor Umar Ascon Passes Away After Long Illness
item.description: Naija News don report say Umar Ascon, the Kannywood actor wey dey well known for him role as Alhaji Lado Mai Barkono for the long-running TV series Garwashi, don pass away after him dey battle long il...

item.title: Automated Blocking of Tumblr in the Philippines Exposes Weaknesses in Legal Oversight
item.description: On March 12, 2026, many Filipinos discovered they could not access Tumblr, as the platform was temporarily blocked nationwide. Tumblr quickly communicated with its users, expressing regret and confirm...

item.title: Four Foreign-Based Players Confirm Commitment to Harambee Stars Ahead of AFCON 2027
item.description: Harambee Stars have scored a major win as four foreign-based players with Kenyan roots have agreed to join the national team. Head coach Benni McCarthy, together with FKF Deputy President McDonald Mar...

item.title: How Misleading Information Fuels Anti-Muslim Sentiment in India
item.description: During a journey from Narendrapur to Patna in Bihar, the author observed the everyday life of Muslims and encountered casual remarks from a driver hinting at mistrust towards the community. This refle...

item.title: Iran urges India to take active role in BRICS amid West Asia conflict
item.description: Iranian leaders have reportedly urged Indian leadership to mobilise BRICS and take a constructive role in the escalating conflict with the US and Israel. During phone calls on March 12, 2026, Iranian ...

item.title: Election Commission to Declare Schedule for Assembly Polls in West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Puducherry
item.description: The Election Commission of India is set to announce the schedule for assembly elections in five states and union territories: West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Puducherry. The announcement i...

item.title: Army and Police Emphasise Co-operation in Crime-Fighting Operations
item.description: Top South African army generals have publicly dismissed claims of a so-called 'turf war' with the police regarding the deployment of 2,000 troops to assist in crime hotspots across the country. Addres...

item.title: Dado Banatao: Filipino Innovator Behind Modern Computing and AI Foundations
item.description: Dado Banatao, a Filipino engineer from Iguig, Cagayan, played a pivotal role in shaping the hardware foundations that made today’s artificial intelligence possible. He passed away on December 25, 2025...

item.title: iFixit Teardown Gives Apple's New MacBook Neo a 6/10 Repairability Score, Best in 14 Years
item.description: Apple's recently launched MacBook Neo, positioned as the company's most affordable laptop starting at $599, has received praise from repair experts at iFixit for significant improvements in repairabil...

item.title: Steven Dubois Defends 500m World Short Track Title in Montreal
item.description: Steven Dubois of Terrebonne, Quebec, captured gold in the men's 500-metre at the ISU Short Track World Championships held in Montreal, successfully defending the title he won at last month's Milano-Co...

item.title: US General Pledges Transparent Investigation into Iranian School Missile Strike
item.description: A US investigation into the Tomahawk missile strike on a girls' school in Minab, southern Iran, which resulted in at least 168 deaths, primarily schoolgirls, is currently underway. General David Petra...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Pushes Freight Costs Up, Pakistan Trade Faces Challenges
item.description: Karachi-based industry experts and traders are expressing concern over the impact of the US-Israel war on Iran on Pakistan’s trade and supply chains. While pharmaceutical and textile sectors report no...

item.title: German Philosopher Jürgen Habermas, Key Figure in Post-War Discourse, Passes Away
item.description: Jürgen Habermas, a towering figure in German philosophy and a profound influence on the nation’s post-war identity, has passed away at the age of 96. Known for his groundbreaking theory of political c...

item.title: Kelly McGillis: From Hollywood Stardom to a Life of Recovery and Advocacy
item.description: Kelly McGillis rose to fame in the 1980s as a leading Hollywood actress, best known for her roles in Top Gun, Witness, and The Accused. She was celebrated for her talent and on-screen chemistry with s...

item.title: Eastern Cape ANC councillor under scrutiny for extended rent-free chalet stay
item.description: A ward councillor from the Eastern Cape, representing the ANC, is facing scrutiny after reports emerged that he has allegedly stayed rent-free for three years in a beachfront chalet at the Gonubie Car...

item.title: EKEDC Schedules Three-Day Power Interruption in Lekki for Network Upgrade
item.description: Eko Electricity Distribution Company (EKEDC) don announce say dem go cut light for Lekki area for three days, from March 13 to 15, 2026, between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. each day. According to the notice wey...

item.title: Briatore Confirms Alpine F1 Stake Talks with Mercedes, Not Toto Wolff
item.description: Flavio Briatore, acting as the de facto boss of Alpine F1, confirmed that Mercedes is in discussions to acquire a 24% stake in the Renault-owned team. Briatore stressed that the talks are with Mercede...
